authors_affiliation: Halla Sigurthorsdottir, Jerome Van Zaen, Ricard Delgado-Gonzalo, Mathieu Lemay — Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM), Neuchatel, Switzerland

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (full test set score, following official ranking of 24th out of 41)

self_reported_metrics.other: Challenge score of 0.511 on the hidden validation set (with optimized threshold 0.3 and TTA); local validation score progression: 0.522 baseline, 0.546 with TTA, 0.573 with optimized threshold; training set score 0.451. Submitting with default threshold 0.5 on hidden validation gave only 0.313.

model.architecture_details: Convolutional recurrent neural network (CRNN): convolutional block of 5 mini-blocks (3 conv1d layers each, ReLU, skip connections, dropout, ~15 conv layers total, optimized to 16-32-64 filters per mini-block) with global average pooling, feeding a bi-directional GRU (128 hidden units) with input/recurrent dropout, followed by an attention-with-context layer, batch normalization, and a final dense sigmoid layer for multi-label class probabilities.
model.key_novelty: Combines a multi-scale 1D convolutional feature extractor with a bidirectional GRU + hierarchical attention-with-context aggregator over sliding windows, then applies test-time augmentation (10 offset variants averaged) and a tuned decision threshold to improve robustness under the challenge's hardware constraints.

input.leads_used: 8 (12-lead recordings with leads III, aVR, aVL, and aVF excluded as redundant)
input.signal_length_window: Sliding windows over variable-length recordings; random start offset sampled per presentation for augmentation; 10 offsets used at inference for test-time augmentation
input.sampling_rate: Resampled to 257 Hz (the lowest of the dataset's native rates of 257/500/1000 Hz); 100 Hz used during hyperparameter search for efficiency
input.preprocessing: High-pass filter with 0.5 Hz cutoff to remove baseline drift on long recordings; resampling to 257 Hz; per-lead normalization by standard deviation computed over the training set

data.training_datasets: Official challenge training data, split 80%/20% into local training and local validation sets, stratified by source dataset and the least common label per recording; no external datasets mentioned
data.class_imbalance_handling: Grouped 111 possible diagnoses into the 27 scored classes plus one aggregated 'negative' class; mapped SVPB/VPB to PAC/PVC as equivalent; tested (but did not adopt, since none improved performance) focal loss, oversampling, MLSMOTE, and an ensemble of binary/multi-label models
data.augmentation_techniques: Random offset for the start of the sliding window at training time; test-time augmentation (TTA) using 10 different input offsets averaged at inference

training.optimizer: Nadam (Nesterov Adam) with learning rate reduced to 1e-4, selected after comparing SGD, Adam, AMSGrad, and Nadam
training.cross_validation_strategy: Single 80/20 stratified train/local-validation split (not k-fold); epoch with highest local validation score selected as final model; extensive grid search over filters, dropout rates, and decision threshold
training.hardware: not reported (paper notes the challenge imposed hardware limitations that excluded several higher-capacity configurations, e.g. >=64 filters/layer, from being submittable)

contribution.summary: Adapts a convolutional-recurrent architecture (CNN feature extractor + bidirectional GRU with attention) for 12-lead ECG multi-label classification, systematically tuning optimizer, filter count, dropout, and decision threshold, and showing test-time augmentation and threshold tuning meaningfully improve the challenge score under hardware constraints.
